message:
fix(ui/mobile): harden viewport locking, safe-area positioning, and overlay ergonomics

This commit addresses a cluster of mobile interaction and layout stability issues that were causing clipped overlays, inconsistent bottom spacing, and brittle scroll behavior across iOS/Android viewports.

What changed:
- Reworked body scroll locking to use a fixed-body strategy with scroll position preservation in .
- Added runtime mobile viewport CSS variable management in  using  and resize/orientation listeners.
- Updated bottom sheet sizing to use dynamic viewport units with CSS variable fallback in .
- Updated associated test expectation in .
- Repositioned floating and modal UI surfaces to respect safe-area insets and dynamic viewport height in:
  -
  -
  -
  -
  -
- Switched hero section minimum height usage from pure  to +fallback in:
  -
  -
- Consolidated/scoped global article and utility styling in , including:
  - root viewport/safe-area variables,
  - reduced redundant legacy blocks,
  - updated scoped article presentation sections,
  - safer mobile-safe top/bottom behavior defaults.
- Recorded issue-tracking updates in  for completed mobile hardening work.

Why this matters:
- Prevents body-scroll jumps and background bleed when sheets/modals are open.
- Improves consistency of overlay placement on devices with dynamic browser chrome and safe-area cutouts.
- Removes fragile  assumptions that caused clipped content and controls.
- Reduces CSS drift by consolidating previously duplicated style regions.

Validation context:
- Type-check and lint were run prior to commit; no blocking errors introduced in this change set.

files.patch:
@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@ export default function Hero({ stats = heroStats }: HeroProps) {
   return (
     <section
       data-section
-      className="relative min-h-[95vh] w-full overflow-hidden bg-[#020617]"
+      className="relative min-h-[min(95dvh,95vh)] w-full overflow-hidden bg-[#020617]"
     >
       <GlowOrbits />
